reviews.review_text:
Awsome place to stay and enjoy the atmosphere loved every minute
Staff Awsome

Love, love, loved our stay.
Cabin stay immaculately clean just perfect set up itself. Ocean views, close to Tavern. Perfect 😊
Relaxing place.

The standard cabin was definitely not with the $200. Expensive even in the off season. The rooms are very worn down and a bit dirty. Both bathroom and kitchen taps dripping all night. Needs some TLC. Unable to fit a portacot in either of the bedrooms for those with little ones

Visiting from the UK, we booked this park for its location to the seafront. ||We booked a 2 bedroom Sea View Villa - Foreshore and found it to be fine for our 2 night stay. I did see other reviews about cleanliness and although the cabin wasn't spotless, it is a self catered type accommodation and you could tell cleaning was done in-between stays, however, deep cleaning of window seals and door frames isn't but that didn't really bother me. Compared to self catered places in Europe I thought it to be in good condition, clean enough for what is to be expected and plenty of crockery and utensils etc. ||My only complaint would be we booked Sea View, however, how cabin was at the back of the foreshore section with multiple rows of car vans in front of us. To see the sea, you had to go outside, step out of the terrace and look to the left.... so working in travel I wouldn't call or advertise this as sea view as that was disappointing and its not true. At best its partial side sea view with obstruction.

The location was excellent, with easy access to nearby shops and a beautiful beach just moments away. The pool was very good and a great place to relax. The ensuite wasn’t the best, but it was still acceptable for our stay. The staff were amazing—very friendly and helpful throughout. Overall, a very enjoyable experience. Thanks Port Hughes Holdiday Park team!
Great caravan park. Awesome for kids and families. The showers had great water pressure - which is a huge bonus! Clean and tidy. The Port Hughes / Wallaroo area is beautiful.

Great caravan park on Port Hughes Harbour. Had a great swim in the harbour. Fantastic pool in the caravan park and amenities are good too. Short drive to Moonta for shops. Mine museum and train ride in Moonta was unfortunately not open when we visited
This is our second year with all our family. The park is wonderful and staff very friendly and welcoming. Already booked for next easter.

The pros:
The pool, kitchen, and toilets were all well maintained. Likewise the lawned area was maintained and good for a kickabout.
The recommendations:
The playground areas and putt-putt golf are looking tired and exposed. A minor upgrade would bring these back to life.
The site could be better connected for pedestrians seeking to access south terrace and onto the forshore, rather than being forced on a convoluted route via the main entrance or the beach access.
The access across the gradients could be made safer with steps, rather than the gum nut laden dirt slopes.
The cons:
Cabin 14 absolutely stinks. It desperately needs a deep clean. This was flagged, but there didn't seem any intent to do so.
The attitude of a staff member in the entrance was, on more than one ocassion, obnoxious, passive aggressive, and seemingly flustered, most notably around the use of access codes for the gate. They need to chill out.

4/6/26
*I can't speak for patrons of cabin 14, I've never stayed there. I went in once though. It stank. Thanks for the comprehensive reply, it will will no doubt help inform and popularise the google review.

We did the booking 6 months ago, on the advertise was showing that was a beautiful grass spot, and when we arrived on the spot they reserved for us it was a rock/sand,  hard and dry spot on the side of the dump of the all camping park,  so we suppose to stay for 4 days but we couldn't, becouse off the insane small off poo all the time,  make me vomit on the first day, on the second day we leave back to home.
Great park we loved it ! Very friendly staff 👏 we had a powered ensuite site and it was awesome👌
An old park that offers plenty for families. Bouncy pillow, playground, pump track and pool. Amenities are quite old and in need of attention. Close to the beach.

What a stunning caravan park with such a beautiful swimming pool and close to the beautiful beach, such clean BBQ's and so much for children to do in this park it's simply an amazing quiet yet children are happy and enjoying their play even though we didn’t bring children here but l would definitely bring my grandson her next time. Thankyou Port Hughes Caravan Park for such a lovely time.

We called early to ask if we could check in early and they said yes as long as the rooms are available.
After already driving 2 hours, we received a text saying they’re busy and it wouldn’t be available.
When we arrived they were not busy at all. Reception was empty and the park itself was half empty too.
My family had been there the whole week prior and said no one had stayed in any of the cabins.
I arrived at midday and they said I had to wait.
I sat there for 2 hours with my partner and 2 young kids and did not see one person/cleaner attend any of the cabins which means they were ready the whole time.
I went back at 1:50 and they said I had to wait til at least 2 and that they would text me when I could check in.
I did not receive any text but saw them letting people in and walking into their cabins.
I walked into reception, had to wait behind another 4-5 people before they checked me in. Not an apology, not a sorry for the wait. Just seems like a bunch of lazy receptionists that don’t stick to their word and are unable to help a young family.

Then on top of that, one of the nights we were there, we tried to leave to visit the tavern for dinner. The boom gate was stuck open. It was 6:30pm so reception was closed. If you don’t put in your code when leaving then you can’t re-enter, so I entered the code and the gate closed on us, locking us in. Tried pressing the numbers on the entry gate on the other side but mustn’t have worked because it didn’t detect a car. Tried calling and couldn’t find a number that would answer.

Have visited the last 3-4 years with about 15 family members but I doubt we will return.

The men’s shower block was the dirtiest big 4 I have been in there’s ingrained and loose dirt in corners and edges of the shower I dropped my soap in the corner and it was like sand paper took me forever to clean it The men’s entrance door wher you push the door open has a 30 cm circle of human grease This has never been cleaned and if they are then  I’m sure management never check it. They were not cleaned while we were there for 4 days. On arrival I went to ask a question and was told were fully booked and handed a  map Never to return

Great place to stay for a few days.
Amazing acces to the beach, clean facilities, excelent kitchen.
I only could recomend to add green mats or carpets to the unpowered spots, as is very dusty.
Great stay.

I had tried to call for 2 day before our trip. The weather forecast for the weekend is for torrential rain, there is even a warning out to avoid travelling through the weather. I wanted to postpone the booking but was told when I finally got through (after trying through multiple different methods) that there was no transfer or refund. I don't think this is suitable or fair.

The dirtiest big 4 ammenities I have ever been in. I dropped my soap in the corner of the shower and when I picked it up it was like sand paper. You can see the ingrained dirt built up along the walls of the showers.|The men’s entrance door has body grease around the door handle & 200 mm circumference when you  push door to open. Your hand sticks to the door yuk|They weren’t cleaned on the Saturday or Sunday when we were there|Most unhygienic|On booking in I went to ask a question and was told we’re booked out and handed a map

There are two sections to this park, the foreshore and the top end, we stayed at the top end. After a hiccup in online accommodation booking we ended up in a lovely 3 bedroom accessible cabin. Having mobility issues this was just perfect as it had a ramp along with stair access. Big 4 however needs to clearly clarify, that when booking online a pet friendly cabin that indicates pets are allowed, that the booking is NOT CONFIRMED, until you contact the park. I booked a cabin as such and then followed up with a phone call as per online instructions only to find out what I’d booked was now not available and had to pay an extra $122 to get a different cabin in a much different location. The park location was set back from the foreshore but within a short walking distance for those able, otherwise it’s only a short drive to the jetty, general store & tavern. The park is well equipped with a a large camp kitchen, swimming pool, small bike/skate park, putt putt golf and designated grass area for dogs. A fully fenced area for supervised dogs would be the icing on the cake. Available to hire, kayaks, surf boards and go carts. Overall a very tidy park immersed in trees providing lots of shaded areas. Staff are very friendly and accommodating.
Good location being close to the beach. Nice pool area but wouldn’t recommend if you’re with young kids as there is no shade around the pool at all or over the playground

We recently stayed in a cabin at the Port Hughes Holiday Park. Of the 7 windows only 3 could be effectively secured, with the other 4 missing the sliding window latch mechanism.  Access to the cabin door was by use of a Restricted Master key yet manually accessing the cabin via windows was simple with the insecure windows which could be opened easily from outside the cabin. This in itself was a concern, not least of all with rooms occupied at night by children.  The interior walls were heavily scuffed and food/drink splash marks on the kitchen and lounge area walls.  The cabin displayed obvious neglect. |I reported the security issues via a phone message to the front office on the following morning, explaining the poor standard of security, providing my name and contact number.  Shortly after I also spoke with a park employee who advised he would speak with office staff.  I received no contact from any staff or management from the park.  This suggests to me serious level of common courtesy, poor communication and problem solving skills, and more importantly, making an effort to provide a secure environment for occupants regarding personal safety and valuable belongings.  I noted other cabins in the park had the same issue regarding the windows so it further questions integrity issues by staff who deal with cabins on a daily basis and what, if any, reporting processes are in place.|It illustrates a business which is more concerned with reduced spending on existing structures rather than implementing regular security audits, especially as other cabins were also affected in the same manner. It was disappointing to witness Big4 treat people in such a manner. Not only for Australian residents, but also international tourists. |I suggest travellers try the cabins at the Moonta Bay Holiday Park.  Any response by the Big4 management will be irrelevant.

We recently stayed in a cabin at the Port Hughes Holiday Park. Of the 7 windows only 3 could be effectively secured, with the other 4 missing the sliding window latch mechanism.  Access to the cabin door was by use of a Restricted Master key yet manually accessing the cabin via windows was simple with the insecure windows which could be opened easily from outside the cabin. This in itself was a concern, not least of all with rooms occupied at night by children.  The interior walls were heavily scuffed and food/drink splash marks on the kitchen and lounge area walls.  The cabin displayed obvious neglect.
I reported the security issues via a phone message to the front office on the following morning, explaining the poor standard of security, providing my name and contact number.  Shortly after I also spoke with a park employee who advised he would speak with office staff.  I received no contact from any staff or management from the park.  This suggests to me serious lack of common courtesy, poor communication and problem solving skills, and more importantly, making an effort to provide a secure environment for occupants regarding personal safety and valuable belongings.  I noted other cabins in the park had the same issue regarding the windows so it further questions integrity issues by staff who deal with cabins on a daily basis and what, if any, reporting processes are in place.
It illustrates a business which is more concerned with reduced spending on existing structures rather than implementing regular security audits, especially as other cabins were also affected in the same manner. It was disappointing to witness Big4 treat people in such a manner. Not only for Australian residents, but also international tourists.
I suggest travellers try the cabins at the Moonta Bay Holiday Park.  Any response by the Big4 management will be irrelevant.

Unsafe, uninhabitable accommodation – refusal to refund. No resolution just a fob off like thier below response.

We booked and paid for accommodation at BIG4 in good faith. Upon arrival, the cabin was not fit for occupation, particularly given extreme heat and the presence of two young children.
The air-conditioning was not operating to a level that made the cabin habitable, and there was no visible evidence of current electrical test and tagging on appliances. These issues were raised immediately with management. After consultation, it was clear the issues could not be rectified within a reasonable timeframe.
Given the conditions, we had no practical or safe alternative but to leave and secure alternative accommodation at short notice and additional cost.
Despite this, management refused a refund. The written response we received from the Holiday Park Manager stated:
“There will be no refund for your cabin as the cabin was checked on your departure day with everything working fine in there. Have a nice day.”
This response is dismissive, inconsistent with our lived experience, and fails to acknowledge basic consumer and safety obligations. Under Australian Consumer Law, accommodation must be of acceptable quality and reasonably fit for purpose. A failure of essential services in extreme heat constitutes a major failure, entitling a consumer to a refund.
We are now escalating this matter to Consumer and Business Services (SA) and SafeWork SA for review of both consumer compliance and safety standards.
This was a deeply disappointing experience, not just because of the condition of the accommodation, but because of the unprofessional and legally flawed response from management when the issue was raised.
Until this is addressed appropriately, we cannot recommend staying here.

Upon arrival, the cabin was not fit for occupation, particularly given extreme heat and the presence of two young children.||The air-conditioning was not operating to a level that made the cabin habitable, and there was no visible evidence of current electrical test and tagging on appliances. These issues were raised immediately with management. After consultation, it was clear the issues could not be rectified within a reasonable timeframe.||Given the conditions, we had no practical or safe alternative but to leave and secure alternative accommodation at short notice and additional cost.||Despite this, management refused a refund. Have a nice day.”||This response is dismissive, inconsistent with our lived experience, and fails to acknowledge basic consumer and safety obligations. Under Australian Consumer Law, accommodation must be of acceptable quality and reasonably fit for purpose. A failure of essential services in extreme heat constitutes a major failure, entitling a consumer to a refund.||We are now escalating this matter to Consumer and Business Services (SA) and SafeWork SA for review of both consumer compliance and safety standards.||This was a deeply disappointing experience, not just because of the condition of the accommodation, but because of the unprofessional and legally flawed response from management when the issue was raised.||Until this is addressed appropriately, we cannot recommend staying here.

we came on the 8|2|2026 and left on the 10[2[2026 stayed in the 2 bed room holiday units all i have to say is get new beds they are old we all had bad sleeps when i got back to adelaide i went to see my chiro  to straight my back  if your charging that amount of money for those old cabins then at least up date the beds those bed are as old as those cabins  franco romano
A brilliant location with great amenities.

Had a really nice stay, the powered ensuite sites were good. LOVED the pool! our only negative would be that they could really do with some shade around the pool area, umbrellas would be nice option.
We will definitely be back! Great location.

Worst place to stay old dirty,
shower broken

no service to fix

Don't bother

The caravan park while it is good, it's not great. It tends to get rather hot and dusty around the powered site areas due to it being bitumen and dolomite. Otherwise, the pool area, and communal kitchen are in really good condition and surrounded by grass, plus easy enough access to the beach via a board walk. Would be nice to see more grass there and some improvements in the shower area.

The air conditioning in the cabin was incredibly poor, especially during a 40 degree day. I was really hoping for a nice chilled cabin but instead it felt incredibly humid inside. And for air conditioning to be installed in the bedrooms too would help with the humidity inside.

Another thing, the mattress' are highly uncomfortable, I slept in the bunk bed room and all I could feel against my back was the wired frames. The beds definitely need an improvement. I haven't been in the other two rooms yet, but I expect the beds to feel the same.

Despite the cabin barely feeling like a nice place to stay in, the pool, beach and giant pillow are quite fun to play around.

Nice place, friendly helpful staff. great value for money. awesome location with great beach access.
My only improvement would be to make the side gate accessible to make access to the shop easier.

Great location for beach access. The standard cabins for 300 a night probably aren't worth the money though. Pool is ok although a little small when packed on a hot day. Lots of excess dirt around the facility. While good access to the beach if you want to walk to the jetty and general store there's a locked gate which means you'll have to walk back and around. Hiring paddle board and kayak are a good price and staff will take them to the beach for you.

The campsite itself was not very suitable for camping as it was extremely sandy and dusty. If you are travelling with family, I would strongly recommend choosing a grassed campsite rather than the red outback dust. Every time a vehicle drove past, dust and sand went straight into the tent. If you have dust allergies, it’s even worse.

That said, the overall camp facilities and staff were good.

We visit Port Hughes with family friends yearly and have done so for consecutive years.  we continue to return for our annual weekly caravan stay due to the enjoyment of the area and the park has great potential; however, with each visit the experience is becoming harder to justify due to the increasing costs and lack of visible improvement.||The sites could most definitely benefit from general maintenance, upkeep and presentation.  the amenities blocks (toilets and showers closest to south terrace) are tired, outdated and in need of upgrades. the Mini golf is long overdue for a refresh, and the swimming pool STILL has no shade and has just gone 6 years since it was installed!||Another disappointing issue during our most recent stay was an issue with the Power box allocated to our site.|Upon arrival at our powered site, both power outlets allocated to our site were covered and unable to be used.  i called the front desk and was advised that this was due to power outages caused by heavy usage the week prior with the hot weather - despite our arrival not being on a hot day, this was still tagged out & we were not advised of this when checking in.|when i called them, staffed informed us that we were only permitted to use the outlet on our neighbouring sites second outlet, or to rely on our caravans power.|This left us with access to only one outlet, and if the neighbouring site required both of theirs then we would have had none.  To make this workable, I had to purchase a power board.||I understand that operational issues can occur outside of managements control, particularly during extreme weather. however, the lack of communication wasn't acceptable.  there was no prior notification before arrival & no reasonable alternative provided.|Further to that, neither of the coax antenna ports worked to use the TV, the fitting simply sat in the hole attached to nothing.||BIG4 Port Hughes has many strengths, which is why we have chosen to return year after year and why this feedback is disappointing to provide. Unfortunately, ongoing fee increases without visible reinvestment, combined with the way service limitations have been handled, have noticeably reduced the overall value of the stay. Long‑term, repeat guests reasonably expect transparency, fair treatment, and facilities that reflect the price being charged.

I was disappointed with my recent stay. The Foreshore park is looking unloved with no visible staff caring for the grounds. Grassed areas were being watered by guests. Garden beds not being watered. Bins almost constantly full. Tried three different shower cubicles at different times of the day and only have 1 out of 5 showers that was warm. Disappointed at not getting vehicle access to the other park with the pool and playground area. So, had to find a park and haul our things in to access this facility.

Playground and bouncy pillow is great and central.
Pool is lovely, love to go to the beach on the walking trail.
We used the ensuite site for our van can't be fault. We will be back. Many thanks

Overpriced cabin at $500 per night. Check in is 2pm but the cabin wasn’t ready at 150pm. Had to follow up with a phone call one hour later, it was then ready. I would expect for the price, for the cabin to be ready on time. I understand the staff are busy, but that isn’t my fault. Walls dirty, floorboards stained. Needs general maintenance, would’ve expected a lot better for the price. Weber was not working as it should, issues with the temperature controls and no bbq utensils in the kitchen.  Reported to reception and someone was going to look at it. Found out later - only when checking out that someone did look at it - no communication about this. ||Nice boardwalk to the beach.

Location is fabulous for a beach stay.
Our accommodation and lack of communication from park management was nothing short of disgusting.
No power in the caravan park itself (for 7 hours) on a 47 degree day, charged $334 for 2A 1C for 1 night in a cabin which was very far from "freshly cleaned".
It took 4 emails, 3 text messages and 4 days before management even responded to our concerns.
The only compensation offered was an embarrassing 10% refund...... remembering we had NO power for 7 hours - 47° -  from mid afternoon through to early evening.
View behind the cabin was an eyesore. Park mostly unkept and looked more like it belonged in the outback rather than directly next to the beach.
The linen on the beds was clean, and the 2 office girls were very polite when under immense pressure from many people when checking in due to the power outage.

Booked in for 1 night 2A, 1C in a standard cabin and charged $334 total on cc (Extremely overpriced for 3 people and the cabin)|Upon arrival on a 47° day, we were advised of no power to the caravan park (they had recently upgraded their own power mains). |This lasted close to 7 hours. |The "freshly cleaned" cabin was a disgrace. The only clean items were bedding which was fresh and comfortable. |Swimming pool area looks very nice but no shade is provided. ||The 2 office ladies were under pressure due to power outage when advised and management failed to respond. |4 days later, 4 emails and 3 text messages later the manager Paris offered a paltry 10% refund. |Remember, NO power for 7 hours and a filthy cabin. |Absolute disgrace. ||The beach however is stunning on a calm day with warm water and safe for children.

I’m hugely disappointed with the Port Hughes caravan park. We booked 2 nights to attend a funeral and decided to stay at the caravan park instead of driving back home because of the exteme heatwave. In 42° the power failed in the park for most of the day and into early evening. It was an exhausting and unpleasant day. We couldn’t risk another day and were forced to leave early. I asked the park to consider a credit due to the lack of power. BIG NO. Absolutely no way they would consider even though they failed  to provide the services we paid for. We are in our 70s. My husband is recovering from chemo and has heart issues. The power failure was in the caravan park only and not a general outage.

Great park and location! 🌅
Only recommendation would be maybe shade over shallow end of pool and some fans in outdoor kitchen area.

Was a very nice caravan park
It was very clean and tidy and our whole family had a great time
Only wish the unpowered camp sites had a mat down so we weren't in the dirt as much, yes we had a sand rug but the dirt would still stick to everything

And my only other complaint would be the pool didn't have any shade over it

Apart from those couple of complaints it is a great caravan park and my kids had a great time on the jumping pillow,skateboard/scooter track, pool, playground, and games area

All the bbqs were clean the showers were clean and its a well maintained park
Such a lovely park for families. Pool, playground, pump track and jumping pillow are great for the kids. We holiday here multiple times a year.

We were booked here from the 2nd - 7th January. We had booked a family cabin in the main area.

Due to how dirty the cabin was we left after one night. It looks as though the cleaners do a light change of bedding, towels and soaps. The floors, kitchen, bathrooms and walls have not been cleaned either lightly or deep cleaned in a very long time. It is the type of dirt that accumulates over a long time.

I would not have stepped foot in the showers and only wore shoes inside the cabin.

During the night a bug crawled into my hair from the bed.
The fridge was missing shelves.
The beds were very very old, hard as a rock and extremely squeaky, you could feel every spring.
The lounges were covered in dirt and spills.
It also stank of sewerage.

We packed up early on the 3rd and checked out. We told the front desk why and they said that’s the way it is (no offer to clean the place), it seemed like a complaint they were all too familiar with. I also texted their welcome message to let them know and their response was ‘thanks for the feedback, I’ll pass that on’.

Looking at their reviews this seems like their standard response to appease their customers, however they then do absolutely nothing with that feedback or change anything to ‘improve’ as their responses suggest.

You absolutely cannot expect people to pay money to stay in filthy conditions. Very disappointed in the accommodation and response from the park.
I had a beautiful stay at BIG4 Port Hughes Holiday Park. Will definitely be back for a longer stay next time. Staff were super friendly and accommodating!!!

This place has gone downt to crap now.
Staff are totally rude,
Camping sites are advertise as green grass, might aswell camp in the dessert.
Facilities are filthy, Broken toilet locks.
They are happy to take your money, but make a complain and they turn their backs, is disgusting..
We almost packed our stuff and left, but didnt want to ruin the new year for our kids.
Everyone on tent/unpowerd sites where not happy, yes we all talk hence the reviews
Caravan Dump point 5 meters away, it smell like boliviar on a hot day..
Please avoid at all cost.!!!!
Please avoid, you will be disappointed

This was our third stay at Port Hughes Caravan Park, and I have such fond memories of staying here. The park has great facilities, including a nice pool, an updated shared amenities building, playground, jumping pillow, carts, and more. The beach is just a 5 minute walk through the dunes.

Our cabin was clean and well-equipped with everything we needed, and the staff were friendly and professional. For those camping in the non-powered sites, they are always green and well maintained, and are conveniently located right next to the pool, toilets/showers, the modern camp kitchen, and the path to the beach.

We will definitely be returning!

I have stayed here a couple of times before-once in the caravan park and it was very good and relaxing.
The facilities were good and I had no complaints.
Because of that positive experience, we decided to try one of the cabins this time.
Unfortunately, we booked last minute and the only available option was a 2-bedroom holiday unit, and it turned out to be the worst stay we've had here. It was definitely not worth the price we paid. All the facilities inside the cabin were very outdated. The beds were not comfortable at all, and while the kitchen had the basic essentials, everything was old and poorly maintained.
Overall, the park facilities themselves are good, and the beach access is great, although the beach was not very clean during our stay, I personally liked Wallaroo than here. It is still a nice place for family time. The newer cabins may be much better, but I would not recommend this particular unit. Management should either renovate these cabins, stop offering them, or charge a much cheaper rate.

Staff were fantastic. Went above and beyond in helping me and family get through a stuff situation.
Great place to visit and would highly recommend to anyone

This is our second visit and sad to say our last.  While the camp and facilities are great and access to the beach couldn't be better, we felt as though we were parked in the middle of a dog park. We are not dog haters but the whole experience has been unpleasant. Dogs everywhere off leads peeing and pooing on and around our site. People repeatedly yelling at their unruly dogs and doing nothing to contain them. An area without dogs would be worth considering.  Anyway too late for us we are choosing not to return, such as shame.

Note: If you stay in the lower part of the park you have to either walk about 1km to the top park to use the pool/playground/mini golf etc or drive and park at reception & walk about 250m through the park. Not nice on a hot day with kids.
So, pay the same amount or more, but have less access to the facilities.
A few days away to relax and go fishing

the cabins were dirty, the lounge had dirt on it. could not get wifi even says free wifi, DVD player didnt work hair dryer didnt work. i think the beach front cabins get all the love. playground needs shade, staff were nice but the park needs a freshen up.

I stayed in the foreshore park.  Clean facilities and close to the tavern and pier for fishing.  Good walking
paths along the foreshore.  Like the place.
Great camp ground with friendly staff.

this is a great Park - have been coming here a few years now with a group BSAC  Adelaide and als used with family . great facility's always clean tidy friendly staff  -
we often come with caravan and have plenty of space - very enjoyable again - until next time
Thank you

We had a great 4 day 3 night stay at the Big 4 caravan park here in Pt Hughes, this park is so big & so very clean & well maintained by the fantastic owners & staff of this Big 4 park . There is a pool putt putt  bike & skate park & the facilities are second to none , I just can't highlight what's all in this caravan & camping park , we will be definitely be coming back 😀 👍👍👍👍👍👍
This was a really awesome place to visit and stay the playground was awesome my kid loved it so much, the walk to the beach was not long at all. Must see
Large caravan park, generous sites. Beach nearby, pool and good kids facilities

I arrived a 12 pm and was turned away as I was early for a ensuit site no mention of this when booking, I returned to check in an hour or so later to watch a receptionist  ,tell and old man that he HAD to use the I pad to check in and when he became upset she said you have to learn every thing is electronic today . Mean while I collected the ensuite key and checked in  I was so disappointed  to see a bathroom that was so dirty it looked like it had never been clean in years the ceiling was  rippled with the moisture it had a vile smell sewerage problem one would suspect there was rust around the shower and the tiles dirty with a lot of scum,the vanity was just holding up made of  chipboard which  water was damaged and im sure it had been repaired many times the floormop was dirty and something was blocking the drain 😳  it was so overall I'd give it a miss,as the staff were also very abrupt and I felt not  approachable at all
Great spot on the beach
This is just an amazing caravan park right on the beach. A great tavern nearby.

We had an ensuite site (7).|Plenty of room, the ensuite was clean ( but could do with an upgrade),I didn't realise how big this park was, the pool and adjoining camp kitchen looked great. Plenty of space.

Quiet as ....
Great country town.Clean ,great facilities.
Reach out and the ocean is there for your boat.
Nice breeze.
Service outstanding.
Port Hughes tourist Park We stayed for 6 nights. Its suited us.

Booked 3 bedroom villa, this is I think our 4th time here. We love it, a great view over the boat ramp and water. Lovely amenities, has always been clean and presentable, showers are lovely. Staff are lovely, never had any issues. Kitchen well appointed, Webber Q was clean and worked fine. BBQ and outdoor seating is weather damaged but to be expected with the abrasive salt/sea breeze. No issues, already planning to come back next year.

Cabins are ok but dated. They say dogs are free which is untrue as they charge you a $50 surcharge and don’t add it to the total when booking online . You have to pay for it when you arrive.
Location is perfect. Tricycle had a flat tyre so paid for something we couldn’t use.
Booked via big4 site then told they don’t honour big4 perks programs ie free bike hire etc
Overall a great park but customer service needs an upgrade.
The ladies in the office Brilliant  and location  was awesome
Took a wrong turn got lost for three ours. Still lost to this day
Great cabins and location, would be a great place to stay in summer with the pool and beach!

Stayed two nights in a deluxe cabin. Cabin was very nice but unfortunately there was NO HOT WATER.
I didn’t contact reception at the time because the staff were not very nice. I told the reception lady on check out about the hot water and she was quite rude and said that I should have contacted them , no apology or anything.
The park they put us in had no playground or anything for the grandkids to do although activities were advertised on the web site.
I paid a premium price as was disappointed in what I got..

Nicely presented Park, very well kept and clean. Shower block was kept fresh and clean. The powered sites had well marked boundaries and easy access to utilities. My only gripe here is that the pitches were too small to fit a medium van, awning and car, and no nearby alternate space for the car. Maybe a metre wider or deeper and it would have fit.
Camp facilities were good, 2 pools, kids play area with a bouncing pillow. A nice outdoor eating and bbq area with a log fire. Also bike hire to hit the nearby Reisling trail. Very short drive to local cafes for breakfast.

Stayed at the powered foreshore part a the park. 2 seperate locations main park approx 500m away staff adjusted our spot because needed big rig space so back from forshore site and just a good a view. nice ammenities and camp kitchen; nice access to jetty passed both Pt. Hughes Tavern and General Store/Cafe
Awesome location and super friendly staff.
Great place in off season, close to jetty and pub

Quite dissatisfied from checking in to an early departure. Gave us the wrong entrance details, didn't advise were to go thoroughly and we got told of for trying to use the wrong boom gate.. reception was quite rude !!
Be careful going in the summer if you stay on the foreshore bottom park.
You need to drive to the kids activities and pool in completley separate location which is not indicated at any point!!
Oceanview cabins on the foreshore!!
The floors were filthy, my feet were black in literally 2 mins...no cupboard space for food if staying more than one night.
Had to move everything to make some room.
No fans to circulate air in the bedrooms. Air cond filter was totally blocked with gunk.. disgusting!
Main bed was as hard as cement, as were the half size single beds.
Lounge was filthy, crumbs, chips,dead flies and bugs ect between the cushions, and they stunk. Wiped one over with a wet wipe and it was black straight away with grime and dirt.. and not comfortable at all.
Don't put to much weight on the dining table, it was almost collapsing. Steel chairs were awful to sit on and rusty.
Only one tiny wardrobe for a cabin that sleeps 5.. one soap, one toilet roll !!
The shower was good though!
So for $240 a night we couldn't sleep, no were comfy to sit, no air circulation, clothes had to be left in suitcases, food left in bags and the hygiene, I have no words!!
We left after one night of 3 we booked as we were so tired and our backs were in so much pain from them cement blocks they call beds and the filthiness made us feel ill.
Will be reporting this to the higher authorities at Big 4 as I am very aware of the standards that Big 4 have to meet and none of them were met, not even close.
Really really disappointing..
Won't be staying there ever again even if it was for free.

Enjoyed a 3 night stay at the foreshore caravan park. The shower and toilet facilities were clean and it was only a short walk to the beach, jetty or general store.

It is a fair distance from the main caravan park where the pool, playground and other activities were though.
Very nice facilities but some areas are a little dated. Still worth it though.
Organised

Unfortunately our stay wasn't great, we felt mislead, the park is actually split in 2 when you opt for the foreshore site which looks like you are staying on the water/view you actually opting for that park, so no pool even though the sign says otherwise out the front  when trying to enter the other park via code to use the pool it didn't work and no one was picking up the phone in the office, and we were put near the gate on a site that gets 24/7 sun not great when you cant have your awning out due to wind, listening to everyone coming and going with the key pad on the site, we booked for a week and left early -please be aware there is one park split in 2
Excellent facilities near the unpowered sites.  Short walk over the sand dunes to the beach.
Awesome
The pool was fantastic. Great location near beach and jetty
Please, if I report something is broken or not working.in a cabin, I expect someone to come and resolve the issue.... Not have to go back and ask again.... Communication seems to be lacking.

Great for kids with nice pool, jumping pillow and play ground.
Very short term walk to nicest beach in copper triangle.
Love the ensuite sites

Stayed in large van site. Across from the pool close to boardwalk to beach!
Very clean amenities, great tavern meals.
Heated pool with great amenities for children.
Definitely coming again !

A mixed review after a 2 night stay. Firstly receptionist could do with a customer service course didn’t find her friendly or welcoming. We had an ocean view unit which was lovely, however no pool!! It’s over a 10 minute walk from one site to the main site to use the pool. The park is in two parts so not practical to say ocean view units have access to a pool. Also no internet as stated. Asked and was told reception had no internet either but no explanation why.

We took our family overall had a great time. Great facilities for the family. We had a site and we got a cabin for our daughter and Partner and 2 kids. Cabin was Very Dirty. They are not cheap $250 a night. And one would expect these cabins to be clean. Stained sheets long black hair and stains on pillows. Hamburger seeds in bed. Sticky bedside tables dirty walls in bathroom. My 10 year old grandson didn't want to sleep in the single beds. They did come reclean room the next day. But no-one wants to sleep or live in someone else's mess.
Stayed for 2 nights in pet friendly sea view cabin no. 1 , what a top spot , great view, lovely cabin , walk to everything , will definately be booking that again , awesome spot 👍

Will not be coming back again staff are rude especially lady at reception unit flooded when having shower. Went up and complained  not only about flooding  but for safety reasons and they just smirked and said we weren't using shower properly.  Really we are not children. Once again very poor attitude towards us will be taking this further some one is going to slip and get badly injured  and also about how we are treated.
Great price even in school holidays and really really pretty
Great.

Staff and people in the ark were great. Pool and camp kitchen is amazing but the cabins are defiantly showing their age. Not a deal breaker but if you like comfort and relative newness she'll out for the premo cabins.
Also, two parks. Minor confusion but easily solved. The kids felts safe,had plenty to do and we had a good time.
Just be never if the cabins had a bit more love. :)

Staff are friendly and it's a great location, however, the cabins (we stayed in a cabin near reception) are a shambles. The beds are old and hard, the recliners are only just functional, the kitchen cabinetry is falling apart.

For the price ($250 per night) i expected more. It's unlikely we'll be back.
Great park awsome for bikes. WATCH OUT FOR SANDFLIES
We paid $285 for a night in the Seaview Cabin.  This was our sea view.

Great camp kitchen and pool area. But the cabin we had (cabin 7) was very dated and for $250/night was very expensive. There were nails poking out the bottom of the kitchen cupboards, the grate in the bathroom sticks up due to broken tiles and the blinds don't cover the windows in 2nd bedroom and TV's don't work very good. The whole cabin needs updating. It's really only worth $15-$160 / night. We were ripped off.

Wonderful park to stay, one of the best we've spent time at.
Friendly staff, clean surroundings & facilities.
Perfect for families with the parks fabulous facilities, eg, the huge pool , large park kitchen  & adjoining alfresco area overlooking the pool area plus attached shower & toilet amenities. It was is so well planned for everyones' enjoyment .
Terrific playground
Beautiful beach with spectacular views & a specially built walk to access.
Highly recommend this park 👌
Such a beautiful place to stay at. Right by the beach and the tavern. Can’t complain about the cabins either.

We stayed 3 nights in one of their least expensive/older cabins. The description on the website was accurate and the photos representative of what we found. Ours had a ramp instead of steps at the front, which was a nice surprise for my elderly father. I noticed most of the others around us had steps so it's worth enquiring about this if you have mobility issues. The cabin was very clean and had everything we needed including linen, bedding and towels. The kitchen had utensils, pots, pans, cups, glasses, etc. All you need to pack when staying in one of their cabins is food and clothing. The cabin had two separate bedrooms and a combined kitchen/lounge/dining. There were 4 large comfy leather recliners and two televisions. The fridge/freezer was full size, not a bar fridge. There was a/c but we didn't need to use this although it looked brand new. We didn't spend much time at the park, other than for dinner and to sleep. We did use their fish cleaning facilities which were very good and included a lot of chopping boards. The pool and surrounding area looked very nice. The staff in the office were very friendly and there always seemed to be a lot of park staff moving around the place keeping busy with cleaning and maintenance tasks. If you need to call the park I noticed they don't tend to answer but if you leave a message they are very responsive in getting back to you. This was my first ever trip to Port Hughes and the only annoying thing is it took me this long to visit. Will definitely be back. Great fishing from the local jetty which is a short drive from the park. We came back home with a freezer full of squid, garfish and tommies.  Very nice looking sandy beach.

Your website is seriously misleading.We booked a cabin based on the pictures on the site and so expected to be down near the water.Instead we arrived to find we were in the middle of a dusty paddock a long way from the water.On a positive note your receptionist was extremely helpful and we paid the extra to be in a Seaview cabin.However it is extremely bad business to mislead people in this way.To be able to upgrade to the nicer part of the park we had to cut our holiday short which was very disappointing

Visited twice for the day and so far what i have experienced and seen, its a nice place to stay, friends stayed many times over the years and i seen the amenities are clean and tidy, access to beach. Short few minutes drive to shopping. Only 15mins to Kadina for the Woolworth shopping and much more. Big Dip, as you enter so be careful slow , you can see many scrapes. Im surprised they have not improved the road entrance. MOBILE PHONE signal with Optus is the best for port hughes despite some thinking telstra is the best. Drakes do sell optus and telstra sim cards but the post office and servo and newsagents only sell telstra and boost which is telstra. Fluctuations optus 1-3 bars signal but telstra 0-2 bars but inside caravan 0-1 and mostly no signal and data internet not possible with telstra but optus was fantastic for data internet and calls fyi. Nice caravan park has a pool and play area. Spacious.

One of our favourite places to stay, now it's become a yearly tradition.

Beautiful park with great amenities. Lots of room for the kids to play and just be kids. The pool with the beach entry is gorgeous.

Staff are amazing and friendly and the go-karts are well kept and in better shape than some of the other parks we have been to - parents will know the pain of pedalling a go-kart that desperately needs a grease!!

Definitely recommend.
Dog friendly cabins, copper triangle townships, Moonta, Wallaroo and Kadina to explore from Port Hughes

We recently stayed for 8 nights in the foreshore park. Very good amenities
Staff were friendly and helpful, the foreshore park is right behind the tavern and general store so very convenient. Not a far drive but a decent walk from the other caravan park section however the staff were helpful in getting us a code so we could visit friends.
Overall a great stay.

I stayed in a cabin with friends last summer. We had a great time. Very relaxing. The executive cabin was comfortable. However I do tend to agree with another reviewer on here about  the so called PET friendly cabins. Believe it or not dogs are not the only pets people travel with. I am a cat owner and would like to see more cat friendly accommodation options. Otherwise don’t advertise your accommodation as Pet friendly as it’s false advertising as your policy only relates to dogs. I would likely go on holiday more often if accommodation providers took cats and other pets into consideration.

What a stellar location! Beach front/jetty front depending on the area you’re located/choosing in the park.
We were lucky enough to go in off peak season. Honestly it was quiet and stunning. I couldn’t imagine being there when it’s full in peak season.
If you’re a beach family, like us, you’ll love it here. Conveniently located for water sport, but also the cheap free stuff such as swimming, and brilliant for snorkeling or simple sup boarding/kayaking and fishing.
Some great walks around, and a cute town center. Don’t miss going through the old cemetery, it’s actually very beautiful and has some amazing stories from the early 1900’s from plagues.
The staff at this caravan park were prompt and efficient, but also flexible and wanting to be helpful. It was a pleasurable experience arranging and extending our stay.
Bathrooms were clean!

Cabin clean and nice spot. 4 sachets of coffee and 4 of sugar. Ran out of sugar after we both had a coffee.  Went to office to ask for a couple more sugars for our morning coffee and was told no, they're complimentary so you don't get anymore. Really? At $205 a night, you'd think they could provide  a bit more sugar!!

Love love love it here... beautiful location, clean tidy ocean view villas. Wish we could live here. Beds are comfortable. Tavern at end of the road does good food. Best holiday. The caretakers of this place are amazing people. Great attitudes and welcoming service

We stayed here with our 5 and 7 year old boys. They loved the pump track, playground, jumping pillow and mini golf. We will go back when it's warmer and try out the pool, which looked beautiful, but it was too cold to get in for me. Good proximity to the beach (via boardwalk) and jetty and pub (via short drive for us but definitely walkable if you have slightly older and/or less whingey kids).
Lovely van park . Beautiful location. Friendly helpful staff. Would definitely recommend and return.

Great rooms and views. Nice staff. Dogs are welcomed but cats are not allowed!
If you're planning to stay here with a dog, you'll have a fantastic experience. However, if you have a cat or other pets, make sure to double-check the specific pet policies before booking to avoid any surprises. I hope the management considers updating their pet policy to be more inclusive in the future.
An absolutely amazing holiday and very well looked after by the staff

First time staying here and will definately be back. Off peak, but very quiet. Check in easy and friendly lady.
We had no matches to light our stove and a SMS to the Reception sorted it and we promptly were delivered some.
The park looks great and would love to bring our grandchildren next time. The pool looks great and easily accessible for everyone. No ladders.
If I had to nit pick the dining chairs could be replaced as they are ripped and worn for an " executive cabin" and the big knife is so blunt we had to use one we bought at Woolies.
Also the bed is a little bit hard but slept ok anyway.
Will definately be back.

We stayed in one of the Ocean View 2BR units. It’s away from the main holiday park but that didnt bother us.
Checkin was quick and easy. Friendly staff.
Unit was clean and fit our family of 5 easily.
Quick walk down to the Port Hughes Jetty as well as having the little playground below was handy.
We drove over (could’ve walked!) to the main park. Played some mini golf and had a play on the playground.
Second time we’ve been here and we’d come back again. Was a great relaxing little family trip.

I had a highly disappointing experience at this caravan park. The old lady at the front office was extremely rude and unhelpful. Her attitude made our check-in process unpleasant and left a sour taste for our entire stay.

Customer service is clearly not a priority here. I would not recommend this place to anyone.

Great location and the beach is perfect. Close walk to jetty and Tavern.
Excellent community pool area and camp kitchen. Great playground area for the kids. Allows camp fires out of fire season
Only downfall would be they require more grassed areas.

Booked an ensuite site but the ensuite was behind the van meaning constantly needing to step over power and waste water getting to and from toilet.  Only one towel rail making it difficult to any towels. Decent water pressure but hotwater a bit hit and miss.  Dirt sites.  very busy on June long weekend meant a lot of young kids with no consideration for vehicles.   Multi family groups take over large areas and Spill into roadways with chairs around camp fires.  Did not feel safe driving at 1 km/hr.

We stayed at the foreshore part of the park, view was great. Unfortunately we had some noisy and rude neighbours who liked to party but you can get that anywhere. Amenities were nice and clean. There is a tavern right in front (Wouldn't recommend the food but close for a drink) and a corner store as well.

Nice quiet park in the off season.
Great showers, clean facilities.
reasonable Optus reception.
$6 washer and $6 dryer(1hr dryer, Should only need one go)
beach access from the grounds, nice walks along the bay.  Not too much seaweed.
bit of a trek to the shops if you don't have transport so shop before you get there.
Local pub is close by for a meal, but its a bit of a double lap getting to it as the closer gate is locked.

Great place to stay, right on the waterfront.
Off peak stay so it was easy to relax without the peak noisy crowds, was so serene.
Love that it's pet friendly too and a quick stroll to dine alfresco with your pooch at The Tavern or the general store.
The villas are fully equipped and spacious, will definitely be back.
Enjoyed .no WiFi was very disappointing.

Nice quiet park while we were there. Plenty for all ages to do, especially children.
Will definitely take grandchildren next visit.
Can imagine it would get extremely busy during holidays.
Great place great views

Great spot. Went again after probably 20 years. The location is great and the pool, camp kitchen and playground are all great. But the sites are all dirt.
I don't know why I was expecting grass, but I was.

Facilities were awesome with large shower cubicles with excellent water flow.
Very nice caravan park, clean well set up, nice pool, walk over board walk to beach, will definitely go back
Lovely place and ocean views

Great park . Right on beach front.
#1 spot if you like fishing. Dog friendly.
Tavern and cafe are great.

Stayed on an ensuite site for awhile. The ensuite was a little dated but adequate.

Staff were helpful when needed.

The parks facilities are quite good, with a  great pool area and camp kitchen. There's plenty to keep the kids occupied and a decent walk over the boardwalk to the fantastic beach.

I would recommend it to anyone to stay here.
Great place

Great family catch up weekend.   So much for kids to do, they are hard to please if they can't find something to do here.  Fantastic pool, great size, I'm hoping they are working on some shade provision for hot weather.
Staff very friendly and helpful.

On the 11th March 2024 I went to another caravan park close by where the management/office staff were rude and uncalm, I left that caravan and came here. What a difference of vibe. The staff here at Big4 Breeze were so friendly, calm and helpful. The man driving around even saw we were heading in the wrong direction and came himself to help direct us. If you are heading this side of SA, this is the perfect place to stay 5 🌟 for staff attitudes 1
Nice park, close to the beach. Helpful managers, clean facilities.

Location is everything
Sadly that is all this park offers
Staff are unaccommodating to any requests
We stayed in a cabin in the main large park
If is an old Jayco cabin run down and way over charged.
Find another park or holiday house

Pro the beach and the jetty area are stunning

Did not stay at this park. Just met up with family.
Our prevouis experience with the so called manager last year was enough to book a holiday house instead.
We go every Easter.....Site on the foreshore is perfect and we have a fantastic time with great friends !
Uncovered grassy site great
Unexplainable experience ! Simply love it ❤️😍 ! Highly recommended

Accommodations in the cabins closest to the boat ramp are good (minus the poorly designed bathroom and shower that leaks water all the way out into the hallway if you don't make a dam out of the bath mats).  But the Wi-Fi here is worthless.  The signal is so weak I got constantly disconnected and it was unusable.  Staff in the office was a bit prickly.
Would be nice if the office stayed open later it was chaos on check-in but I would still go back
A really great place to visit. The beach is fun. The pool is modern.  The cabin could do with a refurb but it is serviceable and the beds were pretty comfortable. We will definitely visit again.

Pet friendly cabin for $250 per night.. Expected more. Very small, run down. Thrown in the back corner of the park with caravan / boat storage. Shower flooded the bathroom. Massively overpriced accommodation. Nice location which I guess is their justification?
Excellent location great facilities
Great place for the family camping

After a long trip on the road, you want a decent place to pitch up an crack a cold one. Unfortunately Big 4 in Port Hughes can't live up to this promise.

While the amenities are good. The inconsistency of the camping grounds and organisation leaves a lot to be desired.

All unpowered camping is unmarked and free for all. In our case this ended up with one family camped on a rocky outcrop more akin to a roadside lay by.

We were lucky enough to get a level site with a bit of grass, but it still had rocks all over it.

After an amazing stay at Renmark over Christmas, which was jam packed, but well marked and organised I can't understand why this place is set up so poorly.

Staff are very friendly and helpful!
Good place to stay Exec cabin
Great beachside location, clean amenities, keeping it simple

Love Pt Huges and Moonta. We stayed here a few times and the ensuites are below standard compared with other parks or even parks half as busy. They always smell of drain/sewage. These pictures are before using the facilities as you can see in poor condition and then mould, hairs on tiles, I purchased an air freshener but it really didn’t work. The area in front of ensuite had been what looked like heavy water and some sort of toilet paper on ground near steps. It’s a shame there’s some good facilities there. We had credit and wife tried booking a specific site in lower park and due to height demand got a different site which we didn’t want and were told no refund or transfer. There goes $140 two nights, won’t be staying again when you can go somewhere else with fantastic service and facilities for a better price.  Also why do some parks even if it’s empty put people right next to you? No it wasn’t busy over stay period.
Looks good, just dropped off a relative, cabin looks very good.

Stayed in an ensuite powered site. Nice clean and modern bathroom with a good size site.
Short 5min walk to Port Hughes South Beach ⛱️

We came in to check in. The 2 ladies there ( one was named Paris, I don’t recall the other ladies name) were so happy and pleasant and helpful. The check in was very easy. We got to our villa, and it was so clean and fresh, and smelled beautiful. The view from our villa was beautiful. We ended up paying for another nights stay because everything was so perfect.

Rented out beachfront cabins 2 years in a row. Beds are pretty average pillows are atrocious unless you like sleeping on a piece of paper. Last year we got there early so thought we would just try our luck and see if the rooms were ready early, were told they absolutely, definitely were not ready as check in is 2pm. A simple no sorry, would have been sufficient.

Next day asked for additional coffee sachets and were told they were complimentary and management had told them that's all guests get.... I'm sorry but for over $800 for 2 cabins for 2 nights, I'm pretty sure you can spare some bloody coffee sachets.

This year, we had one dog friendly cabin, was told that the other cabin had never ever ever had a dog in it and if there was any evidence of a dog an additional cleaning fee would be charged. What's with the drama!?

Also, if you rely on internet for anything and are not staying in the main park area, organise something else because the WiFi is non existent. My phone couldn't  even pick up the park WiFi let alone the tv.

Then we left something behind, I called back on the Monday left a message, no return call. Called today, had to ring 7 times for someone to answer as it goes to message bank after 45 seconds.... got through, got told I didn't leave a message because she had checked them all, wanted $14 postage to send item back. Then when I began providing some 'feedback' about the reception staff, I kept getting cut off and told they were extremely busy.... I'm a sole business owner, I'm busy too!! I love the Yorke Peninsula especially South Beach, but after the atrocious service we've continually received even after spending just shy of $2000 for 4 nights over the 2 years we will be looking elsewhere. Worst customer service in a long time and that's saying something....

Park = 👍

Villa = 👎👎

The beach here is gorgeous and overall the caravan park is nice. Excellent pool and fun play area for the kids. We booked a three bedroom family villa which was disappointing. It’s very tiny, which we expected, but even though it was an 8 bed cabin it only accommodated 6 at the table, which was annoying for our family of 7. (Should one person eat meals on the sofa??) Also, our mattress was the worst—absolutely back breaking. We basically didn’t sleep for the two nights we stayed. The shower was terrible with water spraying everywhere (needs a new shower head). And the gas cooker only had one properly working burner out of four—a tiny one which was hard to manage cooking on as we rotated pots to use the one working burner. Kitchen has the bare minimum. Would be nice to add some basic things like a whisk and strainer. The lounge was also super uncomfortable and old (could feel the springs). If you sorted out the furniture and fixture situation it would hugely improve the guest experience and I’d be leaving another star or two on the review, but as it stands I wouldn’t return unless they could guarantee the horrible old mattresses have been replaced. I can live with other inconveniences, but no sleep on holiday is a deal breaker! It’s a shame because the actual beach is spectacular.
We always ask for the same spot, amenities nice and very clean, lively place to relax, amazing pool area and shirt walk to the beach

Stayed on foreshore grass site. Great spot so much room. We had a beautiful spot overlooking the water with a large grass area. Met great people. Straight below is a dog friendly tavern and general store with takeaway food. Caught plenty of squid - really enjoyed our stay.
Really good 👍
Comfortable place to stay

Beautiful views 😍.

Service was great.

We stayed in a cabin on the foreshore
It's nice to listen to the waves.
First class self contained cabins, even able to have our dog with us.We will be back

Great locale,beautiful cabin,and with privacy. Wonderful kitchen but would be good to provide scissors. No wardrobes seem to be the thing these days, which is weird as the kids room would be very crowded with suit cases on floor etc. In addition the two single beds making one bed is also I thing I have encountered a lot and I'm not a fan, especially when one bed is raised higher than the other as in Rm 11.
Towels thin and no bath mat, this could be better given cost.

Spend two nights. The caravan park is really lovely, very open and has great, clean facilities. It definitely has the best camp kitchen I ever seen. Our cabin was lovely and clean.
My only issue is the bed was so hard that both mine and my husband backs were hurting when getting up.
However overall will definitely go back.

Nice and friendly environment.
Good location.
Fantastic park, comfortable cabin at a good price. Will definitely stay here for my next work trip.
My pet friendly cabin is really nice and comfy. Totally dark outside at night could do with lights along the road. Grit get walked into the cabin unfortunately. So much better than Coobowie.
